items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

articles.title: Victoria ended native forest logging. So where are these trees coming from?

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

image.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

gallery.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

thumbnail.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

items.title: Hobart's Southern Outlet widening project blows out again

children.content: The cost of widening Hobart's Southern Outlet has blown out by another $32 million, with completion of the northern section of the project now estimated at $95 million.

image.altText: Southern Outlet Hobart

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

image.altText: Cross on top of a church

items.title: Union launches bid to save Boag's brewery from closure

children.content: The United Workers Union has launched a petition calling on Boag's parent company Lion Australia to sell rather than close its Launceston brewery. The union has also written to Premier Jeremy Rockliff urging him to intervene.

gallery.altText: a red brick building with grey skies and green signage

thumbnail.altText: a red brick building with grey skies and green signage

items.title: Potential students allege visa scam tied to education consultancy

children.content: Aspiring students looking to study abroad in Australia say they've been left blindsided and with their futures in doubt after an education consultancy promised them migration and visa advice.

image.altText: A graphic pictures a thoughtful woman and text reading 'Visa expiring soon? Discuss your options'.

items.title: Marinus will break energy 'deadlock' hobbling Tasmania, economist says

children.content: An economist says Marinus Link remains an investment in the national interest and will shore up Tasmania's energy security. It follows a report last week questioning the project's business case.

image.altText: An artists rendition of a large cable coming out of a blue ocean.

images.altText: Erling Haaland holds his arms skyward in celebration
Kylian Mbappe points towards the crowd after scoring a goal
Lionel Messi holds his hands up and smiles

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

articles.title: Quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president

children.content: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Merlin the duck, Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe

children.content: Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after inclement weather forces a two-hour delay, while Erling Haaland nets again as Norway takes on Senegal. Follow live.

items.title: Haaland, Mbappe and Messi make it another magic World Cup day

updates.title: 81' Algeria score again through Amine Gouiri

items.title: Photo gives clues to missing man's final hours before disappearance

children.content: A previously unreleased image answers one question in the case of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan, but many others remain.

image.altText: Jack McLennan jumps into Barambah Creek hours before he vanished.

items.title: Perfect dupes of ABC articles are fuelling an industrial-scale scam

children.content: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

image.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

items.title: Toll cap cut, new hospitals and schools in NSW budget plans

children.content: NSW Treasurer Daniel Mookhey has tempered expectations of a return to surplus ahead of Tuesday's budget. Here's what has been promised so far.

image.altText: Cars on a road leading into Sydney, with toll signs above saying city north, city and western suburbs.

items.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports

children.content: WiseTech initially gained ground but is now in the red again after Monday's 18 per cent plunge. The ASX is seesawing between gains and losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

items.title: High cost of living fuels record regional migration, report says

children.content: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

image.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

items.title: Qatar gas complex explosion leaves at least 13 people dead

children.content: The Sunday night blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility is one of the deadliest events recorded in gas industry.

image.altText: The sunsets over power lines and pylons at the Ras Laffan industrial city

items.title: 'A little bit of whining' and other early takeaways from US–Iran talks

children.content: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

image.altText: JD Vance speaks at a podium.

items.title: This artist 'stole' the Rosetta Stone from the UK and took it home to Egypt

children.content: The Rosetta Stone changed the way we understood Ancient Egypt. It sits in the British Museum but this artist wanted to return it home. To pull it off he needed some legal advice, a digital scanner and the courage to take on the way we look at history.

image.altText: A man stands wearing a full plastic mask with just eye holes, in the blue, white and red pattern of large plastic storage bags

children.content: Can you tell these tropical fruits apart? It's not as easy as you think.

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

image.altText: A row of men in suits sitting on a stage

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains a billion people depend on.

items.title: Australians have record-low trust in the United States, new poll finds

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: Potent sedative found in vapes sold in Melbourne

children.content: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

image.altText: An illegal vape.

items.title: Former US Federal Reserve chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100

children.content: The wife of the former federal reserve chair says he died from complications of Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: Alan Greenman wearing a suit with a red tie and glasses while smiling.

items.title: Victorian farm fined for not meeting free-range standards

children.content: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

image.altText: A cluster of hens crowded towards the camera, blue sky and grass behind.

items.title: Reflecting pool to be drained again weeks after multi-million-dollar renovation

children.content: The reflecting pool on Washington's National Mall will have to be drained again after algae and peeling paint appeared weeks after it was renovated.

gallery.altText: Several people holding large poles with one person in the pool and several USA flags behind them.

thumbnail.altText: Several people holding large poles with one person in the pool and several USA flags behind them.

items.title: The hidden dynamic that could undermine global stocks

children.content: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

image.altText: A white rocket launches into the sky on a clear day, with grey and yellow clouds billowing below.

items.title: Children die in hot car as Europe bakes

children.content: At least 18 people died in France, including two children left in a hot car, as a heatwave hung over Europe, smashing temperature records in several cities.

image.altText: People cool off in the Trocadero Fountain next to the Eiffel Tower in Paris.

items.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

image.altText: A front-on shot of a white van driving at night with headlights on and a police van to the right.

items.title: 'More bad news' expected as testing widens after second WA bird flu case

children.content: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

items.title: $800k for mediation not spent prior to fatal stabbing

children.content: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

items.title: Ex-Wimbledon champ handed four-year suspension for refusing doping test

children.content: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

image.altText: Marketa Vondrousova holds the Wimbledon trophy in 2023.

items.title: Australian sprinter feels 'absolutely robbed' after Commonwealth Games snub

children.content: Josh Azzopardi opens up after missing out on selection as a 100 metres runner in Australia's athletics team for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.
